title: quercus phellos var. humilis
intro: Quercus phellos var. humilis, also known as the dwarf willow oak or dwarf pin oak, is a smaller variety of the willow oak tree (Quercus phellos), native to the southeastern United States. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects, which can be attributed to its high tannin content. Quercus phellos var. humilis has been traditionally used to treat various health issues, such as fever, sore throats, and digestive problems, due to its astringent and anti-inflammatory properties. The plant can be prepared as a herbal tea, infusion, or tincture, and is also used in traditional medicine to support overall health and well-being.
